title: Quantum Correlation Hierarchy and Teleportation in Dephased Hydrogen Hyperfine System
abstract: We study the dynamics of quantum correlations in the hydrogen hyperfine spin system subject to Markovian phase noise. Treating the electron and proton spin degrees of freedom as an open two-qubit system governed by an isotropic hyperfine Hamiltonian and local dephasing, we obtain the exact time-dependent density matrix and derive analytical expressions for the full X-state family. We compute concurrence($C$), trace-distance measurement-induced nonlocality (Trace MIN--$\mathcal{N}_1$), and average steering coherence (ASC) in closed form and establish their strict ordering $ C(t)\leq \mathcal{N}_1(t)\leq \mathrm{ASC}(t) $ at all times. Entanglement is identified as the most fragile resource, undergoing sudden death at a finite time. Trace MIN exhibits dephasing-immune freezing for states with nonzero population imbalance, while ASC is the most robust quantity, persisting longest in every scenario studied.We additionally demonstrate that the dephased thermal hyperfine state serves as a resource for quantum teleportation, deriving a closed-form expression for the average fidelity and establishing that the teleportation advantage window coincides exactly with the entanglement survival interval, $\mathcal{F}_A > 2/3 \Longleftrightarrow \mathcal{C} > 0$, for the full X-state family with maximally mixed marginals. We identify four distinct dynamical regimes and map all three correlation measures onto directly measurable Pauli spin correlators, enabling experimental reconstruction of the full hierarchy without full state tomography.
summary: The paper analyzes how various measures of quantum correlation degrade in a dephasing environment using the hydrogen hyperfine spin system. It establishes that while entanglement decays rapidly (sudden death), other discord-like measures persist longer due to population correlations. Crucially, it shows that for standard teleportation protocols, only the initial presence of entanglement dictates whether an advantage over classical communication is possible.
main_problem: The study investigates how different measures of quantum correlation (entanglement, discord-like measures, steering coherence) decay relative to each other when a two-qubit system undergoes local dephasing noise.
main_result: A strict hierarchy $C(t) \le N_1(t) \le 	ext{ASC}(t)$ is established, showing that entanglement ($C$) decays fastest (sudden death), while discord-like measures ($N_1$ and ASC) persist longer due to population correlations.
method: The authors derived the exact time-dependent density matrix for an X-state family under a Lindblad master equation describing local dephasing, allowing for closed-form computation of correlation functions.
model_or_system: The system is the hydrogen hyperfine spin pair (electron and proton), modeled as an open two-qubit system governed by an isotropic hyperfine Hamiltonian and subject to Markovian phase noise (local dephasing).
key_observables: Concurrence ($C$), Trace MIN–N1 ($	ext{Trace MIN-}N_1$), Average Steering Coherence (ASC), and Quantum Teleportation Fidelity ($F_A$).
important_parameters: Dephasing strength ($\kappa$), time ($t$), initial state parameters ($b_1, b_2, b_3$, or purity $\epsilon$)
main_assumptions: The analysis is restricted to the X-state family of two-qubit states and assumes local dephasing noise (Markovian process).
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the time evolution confirming the $C \le N_1 \le 	ext{ASC}$ ordering, showing entanglement sudden death ($t^*$) followed by the freezing/saturation of $N_1$ and ASC at non-zero values.
paper_structure: The paper systematically derives correlation measures for the X-state family under dephasing, establishes their strict hierarchy, analyzes the operational equivalence between teleportation advantage and entanglement survival ($F_A > 2/3 \iff C > 0$), and concludes by proposing a measurement scheme based on Pauli correlators.
why_interesting: This work provides a detailed theoretical roadmap for experimentally verifying the persistence of quantum correlations beyond entanglement decay, which is crucial for understanding resource theories in noisy environments.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
